action.reasoning: Observation: Dual-model (CLIP + ImageNet-21K ViT) features achieved 88.01% test accuracy, up from 83.88% with single CLIP. The frozen backbone approach works well but features are limited to 4 layers per model. Analysis: Using more intermediate layers provides richer multi-scale features at different abstraction levels. A deeper MLP with residual connections can better utilize these features. SWA (Stochastic Weight Averaging) should improve generalization by ~0.5-1% by averaging model weights. Feature noise augmentation during training adds regularization. Decision: Increase layers from [6,9,11] to [2,4,6,8,10,11] (7 per model), use deeper 2-layer MLP with residual (hidden_dim=1536), add SWA for last 200 epochs, add feature noise augmentation, train for 700 epochs.
